platforms.summary: Native-rich governance runtime. Strongest repo and local evidence for skills, hooks, subagents, MCP, and plugin-style installs.

platforms.summary: Native governance runtime with AGENTS.md, project skills, hooks, MCP, sandbox/approval, and officially verified explicitly requested subagent support. Current session may override configured sandbox and approval.

trigger.userTrigger: User explicitly requests agent or a governed task requires it.
trigger.autoTrigger: Meta_Kim may select agent only after Fetch and Thinking identify it as a valid route component.
trigger.configTrigger: codex runtime projection and registry configuration.
trigger.hookTrigger: None unless the runtime maps it to a lifecycle event.

capabilities.notes: Codex support depends on host mode; subagents require explicit request and hooks require trust review.

trigger.userTrigger: Subagents must be explicitly requested by the user, authorized by explicit /meta-theory, or confirmed through a native choice surface that names parallel-agent fan-out.
trigger.autoTrigger: Do not hidden-auto-spawn by default; meta-architecture may fan out only after a user-visible authorization source and Thinking proves independent worker lanes.
trigger.configTrigger: codex runtime projection and registry configuration.
trigger.hookTrigger: None unless the runtime maps it to a lifecycle event.

evidence.verificationCommand: codex --version; codex features list; official docs fetch on 2026-06-15; host spawn_agent evidence must be attached separately before claiming live invocation
evidence.claim: Current Codex releases document subagent workflows, parallel specialized agents, app/CLI activity surfaces, and [agents].max_threads / max_depth concurrency and nesting bounds; live invocation still requires attached host spawn_agent evidence.

capabilities.notes: Codex support depends on host mode; subagents need a user-visible authorization source, concurrent work is bounded by agents.max_threads/max_depth plus task collision boundaries, and hooks require trust review.

platforms.summary: Workspace-oriented runtime. Official docs verify agent workspaces, isolated multi-agent routing, SKILL.md skills, internal lifecycle hooks, and typed plugin hooks; Meta_Kim tool-blocking enforcement remains declarative until a typed plugin adapter exists.

trigger.userTrigger: User explicitly requests agent or a governed task requires it.
trigger.autoTrigger: Meta_Kim may select agent only after Fetch and Thinking identify it as a valid route component.
trigger.configTrigger: agents.defaults.workspace or agents.list[].workspace in openclaw.json / Meta_Kim template.
trigger.hookTrigger: None unless the runtime maps it to a lifecycle event.

evidence.claim: OpenClaw documents agent workspace and multi-agent routing.

capabilities.notes: An OpenClaw agent is a workspace/state/session scope; workspace cwd is not a hard sandbox unless sandboxing is configured.

evidence.claim: OpenClaw documents subagent/background-task concepts, but Meta_Kim still routes durable owners through workspace agents.

capabilities.notes: Treat subagent-like work as partial until the target host exposes an invocation path and verification evidence.

platforms.summary: Official Cursor docs verify rules, MCP, hooks, and subagents. Meta_Kim treats Cursor as a compatible formal projection and keeps arbitrary choice UI as a chat-card fallback until a native popup is proven with strict Cursor test evidence.

codex: Subagents require direct subagent/parallel-agent wording, explicit /meta-theory, or native choice confirmation before fan-out; do not hidden-auto-spawn by default.
Inactive subagent approval may surface in interactive sessions.
Non-interactive sessions fail fresh approval actions that cannot present UI.
Default config is commonly workspace-write plus on-request, but current session may override it.
openclaw: Windows native support should be treated separately from WSL2.
Third-party skills require read-before-enable, allowlist, and sandbox preference.
Register onboard, doctor, and skills verify as verification commands when available.
OpenClaw workspace is the default cwd/context home, not a hard sandbox unless sandboxing is enabled.
Internal hooks automate command/lifecycle events; blocking/canceling tools or messages requires typed plugin hooks.
Meta_Kim currently installs a lifecycle memory hook, not a typed plugin tool-blocking adapter.
cursor: Do not mark hooks or native choice as native without official or local proof.
Treat Cursor as a compatible formal projection; new Cursor-native claims need strict Cursor test evidence, and can merge only after the evidence passes review.
Cursor subagents under .cursor/agents and hooks under .cursor/hooks.json are official surfaces.
Cursor arbitrary native choice popup remains unverified; use chat decision card fallback for human choices.
